summary: Conjecture 1.15 from arXiv:1611.03196 asks for an additive constant c(m) guaranteeing that any bipartite graph G with m edge-sets E_1,...,E_m admits a large matching (of size at least |E(G)|/Delta(G) - c(m)) in which no E_i is over-represented beyond the ceiling of |E_i|/Delta(G). No verified resolution of this conjecture was found in the literature after an exhaustive web search. The conjecture is from 2016 and appears to remain open as of May 2026.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Invertibility of digraphs and tournaments' by Alon, Powierski, Savery, Scott, Wilmer; its topic is the inversion number of directed graphs and NP-completeness of related decision problems. It does not cite or discuss Conjecture 1.15 from arXiv:1611.03196. The fuzzy match (score 80) is a false positive.

search_queries: arXiv:1611.03196 "fair representation" bipartite matching conjecture cite
Aharoni Alon "fair representation" bipartite matching "independent sets" conjecture 2017 2018 2019 2020
"fair representation" bipartite matching "c(m)" "Delta" edges conjecture Aharoni Alon 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
"almost fair" bipartite matching "1611.03196" OR "Aharoni Alon Berger Chudnovsky" arXiv
notes: No verified follow-up paper addressing Conjecture 1.15 was found after 5 web calls. A ScienceDirect paper titled 'Almost fair perfect matchings in complete bipartite graphs' appeared in search results but returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified; it is not included in since_posted. The internal reference arXiv:2212.11969 is a false positive from the corpus fuzzy-matching step. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is approximately 9 years old, making the absence of indexed follow-up somewhat less conclusive.
